questions.question: Why are transport and communication called the lifelines of a nation?
questions.correct_answer: Because they enable movement of goods, services, and information which supports economic development
questions.explanation: Efficient transport and communication networks help trade, connect markets, and support national development.

questions.question: Which are the three major domains of transport?
questions.explanation: Transport systems are classified based on the medium through which goods and people move.

questions.question: What is the Golden Quadrilateral?
questions.correct_answer: A network of super highways connecting Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai and Kolkata
questions.explanation: It was developed to reduce travel time and improve connectivity between major cities.

questions.question: Why are roadways important for transportation?
questions.correct_answer: They provide door-to-door service and are economical for short distances
questions.explanation: Roadways are flexible and connect villages, towns and cities easily.

questions.question: Which mode of transport is best suited for carrying heavy and bulky goods over long distances?
questions.explanation: Railways are efficient for transporting large quantities of goods and passengers.

questions.question: What is pipeline transport used for?
questions.correct_answer: Transporting crude oil, petroleum products and natural gas
questions.explanation: Pipelines are efficient for continuous transport of liquids and gases.

questions.question: Why are waterways considered the cheapest mode of transport?
questions.correct_answer: Because they use natural routes and consume less fuel for transporting heavy goods
questions.explanation: Waterways are economical and environmentally friendly.

questions.question: Which mode of transport is the fastest?
questions.explanation: Airways connect distant and remote regions quickly.

questions.question: What is international trade?
questions.correct_answer: Exchange of goods and services between countries
questions.explanation: International trade includes exports and imports and indicates economic prosperity.

questions.question: What is balance of trade?
questions.correct_answer: The difference between the value of exports and imports of a country
questions.explanation: If exports exceed imports, the balance of trade is favourable.

notes.title: Roadways
notes.content: Roadways are flexible, provide door-to-door services, and are suitable for short-distance transport.

notes.title: Railways
notes.content: Railways are the principal mode of transportation for long-distance travel and heavy goods in India.

notes.title: Pipeline Transport
notes.content: Pipelines transport liquids and gases such as petroleum and natural gas over long distances.

notes.title: Water Transport
notes.content: Waterways are the cheapest and most fuel-efficient mode of transport for heavy goods.

notes.title: Air Transport
notes.content: Air transport is the fastest mode and is useful for connecting remote and inaccessible areas.

facts.fact: India has one of the largest road networks in the world.
The first train in India ran from Mumbai to Thane in 1853.
Waterways are considered the most economical mode for transporting bulky goods.
International trade acts as an economic barometer for a country.
